column1: # i2p rotating tips — one per line, each ≤25 words, no leading "Did you know?" (the hook adds it).
# Blank lines and #-comments are ignored. Keep them honest: reference real, installed capabilities.
/i2p-help browses every power you have now, grouped by the value flow — your front door to the whole marketplace.
/i2p-help explains the product lifecycle — an eight-phase cycle, DISCOVER → … → OPERATE ↻ — and offers to kick one off.
/i2p-lifecycle starts and tracks a product lifecycle; the concierge status line shows the live phase.
/concierge:statusline turns on the status line — gauges, the product-lifecycle phase, and the ⚔ caught reviewer tally.
/i2p-review fans out every installed reviewer — code, design, docs, security — and returns one verdict in I2P_REVIEW.md.
/i2p-check runs all your plugins' dependency checks at once and merges them into a single readiness table.
/i2p-flow draws where each plugin sits from idea to production — and tells you the next command to run.
foundry's /pr-review reviews a diff like a hostile-but-fair senior engineer, then gives one PASS/NEEDS_REVISION/BLOCK verdict.
market-scanner's /goal then /market-scan hunt for a worth-building opportunity before you write any code.
ideator's /ideate turns a raw idea into a build-ready IDEA package — agent-facing docs plus an illustrated dossier.
atelier's /ui-review crawls any running SPA and scores each screen against named design canon and WCAG 2.2.
atelier's /mockup composes polished screens and runs a designer↔reviewer loop until they clear a design-fitness rubric.
security's /scan-all catches PII, secrets, and vulnerable dependencies before you ship — one PASS/REVIEW/BLOCK report.
pressroom's /publish turns your repo into articles, diagrams, or a print-quality PDF with legible figures.
Every plugin stands alone and lights up the others automatically when installed — no hard dependencies in any direction.
The whole suite is self-improving: a lesson learned folds back via PR, so every user inherits the fix.
